_comment: SafeSea regulations feed. Edit values when NOAA changes them, host this file at a public URL (e.g. GitHub raw), and paste that URL into the app Regs tab > Auto-Update Feed. The app merges these over its built-in data on launch.

rec.size: Two keeper ranges: 27–47" and 47–<73" CFL. 73"+ is a separate “trophy” fishery (see below).
rec.limit: Private: 2 per vessel per day/trip — only 1 may be 47–<73"; the other must be 27–<47". Charter/Headboat: 3. Per vessel, NOT per angler.
notes: No targeted bluefin in the Gulf of America (spawning grounds).
You cannot keep ANY bluefin if a hammerhead shark is aboard or was offloaded.
Report every bluefin kept or discarded dead within 24 hrs (HMS Permit Shop, the catch-report app, or 888-872-8862).

charter.size: Two keeper ranges: 27–47" and 47–<73" CFL. 73"+ is a separate “trophy” fishery (see below).
charter.limit: 3 per vessel per day/trip when fishing recreationally (27–<73"). With a commercial sale endorsement you may instead fish that day under General-category commercial rules — the size of the FIRST bluefin you keep decides the mode (see below).
charter.permit: HMS Charter/Headboat permit. Report every bluefin kept or discarded dead within 24 hrs.
notes: The first bluefin you retain sets the day’s mode: keep a 73"+ fish first → commercial (General-category) rules & limits apply for the whole day; keep a 27–<73" fish first → recreational (3-fish Angling) rules apply. You cannot mix the two in one day.
Commercial mode also requires a commercial sale endorsement and selling only to a permitted dealer (see the Commercial tab).
No targeted bluefin in the Gulf of America.
No bluefin may be kept if a hammerhead shark is aboard.
For-hire vessels must file electronic trip reports (eVTR).

comm.size: Commercial sale: 73"+ only. Head-off for sale must be ≥54" pectoral-fin CFL.
comm.limit: Set in-season by NOAA; varies by category & time period — full General-category schedule below.
comm.permit: Atlantic Tunas permit — General, Harpoon, Trap, Purse Seine, or Longline category (or HMS Charter/Headboat w/ commercial sale endorsement). Sell only to permitted dealers; daily catch reporting required.
notes: Each General-category time period (Jan–Mar, Jun–Aug, Sep, Oct–Nov, Dec) closes the instant its subquota is hit.
Harpoon category (Jun 1–Nov 15, 2026): up to 5 fish 73"+ per vessel/day.
Tag-and-release of all sizes is still allowed during commercial closures.

rec.permit: Federal HMS Angling permit (some states also require a recreational offshore landing permit).
notes: Speargun-caught Atlantic tunas may not be sold.

charter.limit: 3 per person per day (default) when fishing recreationally.
charter.permit: Federal HMS Charter/Headboat permit. For-hire eVTR trip reporting required.
notes: With a commercial sale endorsement, selling tunas follows commercial rules.

comm.size: 27" CFL min (head-off for sale must leave ≥27" from tail fork to the cut).
comm.limit: No federal daily trip limit for permitted vessels (market/handling limits apply) — follow the current HMS Commercial Compliance Guide.
comm.permit: Atlantic Tunas permit (commercial). Sell only to permitted dealers.
notes: ICCAT-managed; landings monitored and rules can change in-season.

rec.limit: 3 per person per day (the rec tuna limit covers bigeye, yellowfin & albacore).
notes: Same handling & reporting framework as other Atlantic tunas.

charter.permit: Federal HMS Charter/Headboat permit. For-hire eVTR reporting required.
notes: Same handling & reporting framework as other Atlantic tunas.

comm.limit: No federal daily trip limit for permitted vessels — follow the HMS Commercial Compliance Guide.
comm.permit: Atlantic Tunas permit (commercial). Sell only to permitted dealers.
notes: ICCAT-managed; quotas can shift year to year.

rec.size: No federal minimum in the Mid-Atlantic/Greater Atlantic. (FL Atlantic: 20" fork; U.S. Caribbean: 24" fork.)
rec.limit: Atlantic federal: 10 per person/day, max 54 per vessel/day (headboats exempt from the vessel cap). FL Atlantic is stricter: 5/person, 30/vessel, no captain/crew limit.
rec.permit: No HMS permit (managed by the regional councils). State saltwater license as required.
notes: Must be landed with head and fins intact.
State rules (especially Florida) can be tighter than federal — check where you land.

charter.size: No federal Mid-Atlantic minimum. (FL Atlantic: 20" fork.)
charter.limit: 10 per person/day; charter vessel cap 54 (headboats exempt from the cap). FL Atlantic for-hire: 5/person.
charter.permit: No HMS permit; council-managed. For-hire vessels need the federal Dolphin/Wahoo for-hire permit & must file trip reports.
notes: Must be landed with head and fins intact.
State rules (esp. FL) can be tighter — check where you land.

comm.size: No federal Mid-Atlantic minimum; regional rules vary.
comm.limit: Commercial trip limits and an annual catch limit apply — the fishery closes when the ACL is reached. Check SAFMC/NOAA bulletins.
notes: Sold only through licensed dealers.

rec.size: 54" fork length minimum for most authorized species (no minimum for Atlantic sharpnose & bonnethead).
rec.limit: 1 shark per VESSEL per trip (authorized species), PLUS 1 Atlantic sharpnose AND 1 bonnethead per PERSON per trip.
rec.permit: HMS Angling permit WITH the free Shark Endorsement (online ID/handling course). Non-offset, non-stainless circle hooks required when fishing for sharks south of 41°43′N (except flies/lures).
notes: SHORTFIN MAKO: retention is ZERO — release it, dead or alive.
Land sharks whole with fins naturally attached.
No bluefin tuna may be kept if any hammerhead is aboard.

charter.size: 54" fork length min for most species (none for Atlantic sharpnose & bonnethead).
charter.limit: 1 shark per VESSEL per trip, PLUS 1 Atlantic sharpnose AND 1 bonnethead per PERSON.
charter.permit: HMS Charter/Headboat permit WITH the Shark Endorsement. Circle-hook rule applies south of 41°43′N. For-hire eVTR reporting required.
notes: SHORTFIN MAKO: retention ZERO — release dead or alive.
Land sharks whole with fins naturally attached.

comm.size: Varies by complex; many large coastal sharks 54"+ — see the HMS Commercial Compliance Guide.
comm.limit: Retention limits are set by species complex & region and adjusted in-season (e.g. large-coastal-shark trip limits); complexes open and close by quota.
comm.permit: Atlantic Shark Directed or Incidental limited-access permit (or Smoothhound open-access). Sell only to permitted dealers.
notes: Shortfin mako retention is ZERO in commercial fisheries too.
The prohibited-species list below applies to commercial too.
